TGNMember.title: TGNMember
TGNMember.description: Represents a member of the Transcend Global Network.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TGN Member.

tgnMemberId.description: Unique TGN Member ID assigned during onboarding.

sectorPreferences.description: List of sector preferences for the member.

programIds.description: References to Programs. (Relationship: TGNMember N:N Program)

communityIds.description: References to Communities. (Relationship: TGNMember N:N Community)

eventIds.description: References to Events. (Relationship: TGNMember N:N Event)

isMenteeVerified.description: Indicates if the mentee is verified.

MentorCertification.title: MentorCertification
MentorCertification.description: Represents the certification status of a mentor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Mentor Certification.

programCompletion.description: Indicates if the mentor has completed the certification program.

isAssociateVerified.description: Indicates if the mentor (Associate) is verified.

LearningHistory.title: LearningHistory
LearningHistory.description: Represents the learning history of a member.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Learning History entry.

Program.title: Program
Program.description: Represents a program offered by the network.

memberIds.description: References to TGNMembers. (Relationship: Program N:N TGNMember)

Community.title: Community
Community.description: Represents a community within the network.

memberIds.description: References to TGNMembers. (Relationship: Community N:N TGNMember)

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents an event organized by the network.

dateTime.description: Date and time of the event.

memberIds.description: References to TGNMembers. (Relationship: Event N:N TGNMember)

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents a course offered on the platform.

AffiliateReferral.title: AffiliateReferral
AffiliateReferral.description: Represents a referral in the affiliate income system.

referrerMemberId.description: Reference to the TGNMember who made the referral. (Relationship: AffiliateReferral 1:N TGNMember)

referredMemberId.description: Reference to the TGNMember who was referred. (Relationship: AffiliateReferral 1:N TGNMember)

MarketplaceListing.title: MarketplaceListing
MarketplaceListing.description: Represents a listing in the e-commerce marketplace.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Marketplace Listing.

sellerMemberId.description: Reference to the TGNMember selling the item. (Relationship: MarketplaceListing 1:N TGNMember)

approvalStatus.description: Approval status of the listing (e.g., pending, approved, rejected).

packageBenefitIds.description: References to PackageBenefits. (Relationship: MarketplaceListing N:N PackageBenefits)

PackageBenefits.title: PackageBenefits
PackageBenefits.description: Represents package benefits for marketplace listings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Package Benefit.

definition.description: Stores TGNMember data, using the user's ID as the document ID for easy access control.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to their Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores the learning history of a member, nested under the user's document. This enables easy management and querying of learning data per user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: learningHistoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the learning history entry.

definition.description: Stores mentor certifications. The document ID is the mentorCertificationId

params.name: mentorCertificationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the mentor certification.

definition.description: Stores program information.  No specific authorization context denormalized here; access is likely managed via membership in the TGNMember document.

params.name: programId
params.description: The unique identifier of the program.

definition.description: Stores community information. No specific authorization context denormalized here; access is likely managed via membership in the TGNMember document.

params.name: communityId
params.description: The unique identifier of the community.

definition.description: Stores event information. No specific authorization context denormalized here; access is likely managed via membership in the TGNMember document.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier of the event.

definition.description: Stores course information. No specific authorization context denormalized here; access is likely managed via membership in the TGNMember document.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the course.

definition.description: Stores affiliate referral data.  No direct user ownership; access control would likely depend on admin roles or other factors outside user control.

params.name: affiliateReferralId
params.description: The unique identifier of the affiliate referral.

definition.description: Stores marketplace listings. The `sellerMemberId` field is used for authorization to control access based on ownership.

params.name: marketplaceListingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the marketplace listing.

definition.description: Stores package benefits for marketplace listings.  No specific authorization context denormalized here; access is likely managed via admin roles.

params.name: packageBenefitId
params.description: The unique identifier of the package benefit.

definition.description: Stores sector information.  No specific authorization context denormalized here; access is likely managed via admin roles.

params.name: sectorId
params.description: The unique identifier of the sector.

firestore.reasoning: This Firestore structure prioritizes authorization independence and QAPs through denormalization. User-owned data is stored in hierarchical paths under the `/users/{userId}` collection to simplify ownership-based security rules. Collaborative data uses membership maps for authorization. Each document includes necessary authorization context (e.g., `members` map) to avoid `get()` calls in security rules. This supports atomic operations and makes debugging easier. The segregation of data based on access control needs further simplifies the rules.
